summary.migrationThesis: Migrate high-signal workbench chrome first, then replace residual buckets with abstract chrome tone roles or bounded compatibility exceptions.

chrome-surface.label: Chrome Surface
chrome-surface.suggestion: candidate chrome tone roles like shell / shellRaised / shellInset
chrome-surface.rationale: Residual shell backgrounds and borders still express platform-local elevation and should be grouped before further migration.

chrome-ink.label: Chrome Ink
chrome-ink.suggestion: candidate chrome tone roles like shellInk / mutedInk / subtleInk
chrome-ink.rationale: Foreground and supporting text hierarchy in the shell should become reusable tone roles before adding more platforms.

feedback-status.label: Feedback and Status
feedback-status.suggestion: candidate feedback roles like error / warning / info / success
feedback-status.rationale: Validation, git, and attention colors should move through abstract feedback roles instead of platform-local key ownership.

selection-list-state.label: Selection and List State
selection-list-state.suggestion: candidate navigation-state roles for active / inactive / focus list treatments
selection-list-state.rationale: List selection states are shared navigational behavior and should be expressed as abstract navigation roles, not raw VS Code keys.

scrollbar-shadow.label: Scrollbar and Shadow
scrollbar-shadow.rationale: Scrollbars and drop shadows are renderer-specific polish details and can remain as bounded platform compatibility values.

terminal-ansi.label: Terminal ANSI
terminal-ansi.suggestion: keep platform-specific for now, later map through terminal semantic roles
terminal-ansi.rationale: ANSI slots are terminal-specific compatibility outputs and should migrate only after a terminal semantics layer exists.

entries.suggestion: candidate chrome tone roles like shell / shellRaised / shellInset
entries.rationale: Residual shell backgrounds and borders still express platform-local elevation and should be grouped before further migration.

entries.suggestion: keep platform compatibility for now
entries.rationale: Scrollbars and drop shadows are renderer-specific polish details and can remain as bounded platform compatibility values.

chrome-surface.label: Chrome Surface
chrome-surface.suggestion: candidate chrome tone roles like shell / shellRaised / shellInset
chrome-surface.rationale: Residual shell backgrounds and borders still express platform-local elevation and should be grouped before further migration.

chrome-ink.label: Chrome Ink
chrome-ink.suggestion: candidate chrome tone roles like shellInk / mutedInk / subtleInk
chrome-ink.rationale: Foreground and supporting text hierarchy in the shell should become reusable tone roles before adding more platforms.

feedback-status.label: Feedback and Status
feedback-status.suggestion: candidate feedback roles like error / warning / info / success
feedback-status.rationale: Validation, git, and attention colors should move through abstract feedback roles instead of platform-local key ownership.

selection-list-state.label: Selection and List State
selection-list-state.suggestion: candidate navigation-state roles for active / inactive / focus list treatments
selection-list-state.rationale: List selection states are shared navigational behavior and should be expressed as abstract navigation roles, not raw VS Code keys.

scrollbar-shadow.label: Scrollbar and Shadow
scrollbar-shadow.rationale: Scrollbars and drop shadows are renderer-specific polish details and can remain as bounded platform compatibility values.

terminal-ansi.label: Terminal ANSI
terminal-ansi.suggestion: keep platform-specific for now, later map through terminal semantic roles
terminal-ansi.rationale: ANSI slots are terminal-specific compatibility outputs and should migrate only after a terminal semantics layer exists.
